summary: Remove ElectionInProgress (216) from ResumableChangeStreamError
Don't use admin database for FLE tests
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    SERVER-35804 has made it invalid to drop collections on the admin database in a sharded cluster, which has caused FLE tests to fail with duplicate key errors on the "latest" server variant due to step 2 in [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/client-side-encryption/tests#use-as-integration-tests]

Maybe we can get around this by doing a deleteMany({}) instead of a drop?

CC [~kevin.albertson]
    See DRIVERS-1194 for updated details.

summary: Remove geoHaystack and geoSearch
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
        {panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    geoHaystack and geoSearch are now deprecated
    {panel}
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    null
    
    See DRIVERS-987 for updated details.

summary: Remove command method from Topology
description: A {{Topology}} is meant to be a means of selecting servers to run a command against, the {{command}} helper function introduces confusion to our tests and internal code and should be removed.

summary: Ensure that the WriteConcernError "errInfo" object is propagated
description: See DRIVERS-967 for details.

summary: SDAM test typo "compatible"
description:
Here "comptabile" should be "compatible"

[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.6/test/unit/sdam/spec.test.js#L226]

summary: Make tryNext public
description:
Hi, the ChangeStream class has been updated so that we can no longer access the internal ChangeSteamCursor in order to call tryNext. Please add a method to the ChangeStream class so that we can use the tryNext method on changeStreams.

 

Additionally, tryNext on other cursors was marked as internal (specifically: the tryNext method that does not take a callback. The one that takes a callback is accessible).

summary: initialising change stream results in: TypeError: Cannot read property 'documents' of null
description:
This happened when initialising a change stream using a resume token (via `startAfter` option).

full stack trace:
{code}
TypeError: Cannot read property 'documents' of null
 at /app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/change_stream.js:307:31
 at handleCallback (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:32:5)
 at /app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:685:38
 at /app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:694:39
 at ChangeStreamCursor._endSession (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:395:7)
 at _setCursorNotifiedImpl (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:694:10)
 at setCursorNotified (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:685:3)
 at done (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:451:16)
 at queryCallback (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:500:18)
 at /app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/cursor.js:550:9
 at executeCallback (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/operations/execute_operation.js:70:5)
 at callbackWithRetry (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/operations/execute_operation.js:118:14)
 at /app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/operations/command_v2.js:90:9
 at /app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cmap/connection_pool.js:352:13
 at handleOperationResult (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/server.js:489:5)
 at MessageStream.messageHandler (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cmap/connection.js:270:5)
 at MessageStream.emit (events.js:209:13)
 at processIncomingData (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cmap/message_stream.js:144:12)
 at MessageStream._write (/app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/cmap/message_stream.js:42:5)
 at doWrite (_stream_writable.js:428:12)
 at writeOrBuffer (_stream_writable.js:412:5)
 at MessageStream.Writable.write (_stream_writable.js:302:11)
 at TLSSocket.ondata (_stream_readable.js:722:22)
 at TLSSocket.emit (events.js:209:13)
 at addChunk (_stream_readable.js:305:12)
 at readableAddChunk (_stream_readable.js:286:11)
 at TLSSocket.Readable.push (_stream_readable.js:220:10)
 at TLSWrap.onStreamRead (internal/stream_base_commons.js:182:23)
{code}

I tried to debug it by placing console.log in various places and comparing it to a case in which the change stream initialised without issues. In the case which lead to the above error the cursor returned by initial aggregation (logged [here|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/fa07519bf0b1d2278c0c824e6471c62267c652ef/lib/cursor/core_cursor.js#L485]) operation didn't have postBatchResumeToken property and had id = Long(0):
 
{code:java}
{
  firstBatch: [],
  id: Long { _bsontype: 'Long', low_: 0, high_: 0 },
  ns: 'my-db.my-collection'
}
{code}


using:
mongodb@35.7
node@12.14.0

summary: Make BulkWriteError conform to CRUD spec
description: https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/2005#discussion_r288315978

summary: Connections can be leaked if wait queue members are cancelled
description: There is a bug in the new CMAP implementation where connections may be leaked if a wait queue member times out before execution. The bug is exacerbated by using a {{waitQueueTimeoutMS}} (and would likely have surfaced as part of client-side operation timeout), but only occurs when there are no active connections, the pool is not maxed out, and the wait queue member times out between checkout and connection establishment.

summary: Support readConcern on Cursor
description:
Looking for a driver equivalent to 
[https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/method/cursor.readConcern|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/method/cursor.readConcern/]

summary: Use ReadPreference.mode for command query construction
description:
[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.5/lib/core/wireprotocol/command.js#L73]

This is checking for a property {{preference}} on a {{ReadPreference}} instance, and it should be looking for {{.mode}}

summary: Server monitoring is prevented under heavy request load
description: In simulated cases of heavy load (writes occurring every 1-10ms) it is possible to prevent the rescheduling of server monitoring checks. In this scenario, the logic to debounce calls to monitoring within {{minHeartbeatFrequencyMS}} is tying up the event loop and preventing future monitoring attempts from being scheduled.

summary: Raise error if hint specified for unacknowledged update using OP_MSG or OP_UPDATE
description: See DRIVERS-790 for details.

summary: Migrate write operations to executeOperation
description: Retryability for write operations in the driver currently uses a different code path than retryable reads, which makes the codebase more difficult to maintain (and in this case, add type information to). We should use {{executeOperation}} exclusively for reads and writes.

summary: Reimplement AsyncIterator for Cursor optionally using a provided promise implementation
description: A recent [change|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/commit/3ae3e91935f2252bb74d617317b11433f5896867] removed our custom implementation for {{AsyncIterator}} on the cursor type. While this fixes a bug by bringing our cursor in line with the {{Readable}} async iterator implementation, it also drops support for using a custom promise library passed into the driver. We should reimplement this, and use the solution Thomas is developing in his "global promise" feature.

summary: Remove CommandResult
description: Some of our wire protocol methods return the raw result from the server, and others return a {{CommandResult}}. This type prevents any hope we have of maintaining abstractions between the wire, command and operation layers, and should be removed

summary: unify means of applying `ignoreUndefined`
description:
{{ignoreUndefined}} is applied to options [in|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/collection.js#L457] [a|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/collection.js#L727] [number|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/collection.js#L755] [of|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/collection.js#L727] [places|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/collection.js#L755] (and thats not even all of them!). It's also noticeably missing from relevant functions (such as all of the {{findAndModify}} methods, as noted in NODE-1560).

We should reduce this code some common method that applies to options, reducing code where its already duplicated, and adding it to methods that require the check.

summary: Driver support for server Hedged Reads
Testing Data Lake with Drivers
description: See DRIVERS-829 for updated details.

summary: Ensure ConnectionCreatedEvents are emitted before ConnectionReadyEvents
description: See DRIVERS-1042 for updated details.

summary: Update documentation for Text Search
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Now that we have $search for Atlas, we should update all the drivers documentation that we own to mention this and if possible, include an example.


    See DRIVERS-1227 for updated details.

summary: Query parameters with path in connection string not working on windows
description:
h3. Problem Description

Some of the query parameters in a connection string allow a file path to be specified, e.g. the {{tlsCAFile}} option, that will be resolved to the file contents before connecting. However, using an absolute path on Windows will result in an error:

{{TypeError [ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE]: The "path" argument must be of type string or an instance of Buffer or URL. Received an instance of Object}}

It seems that the error is caused by the {{parseQueryStringItemValue}} method ([https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/src/connection_string.ts#L154)|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/src/connection_string.ts#L154).]. It checks for the presence of a {{:}} in the parameter value and if so converts it into an object with key-values by splitting. However, an absolute path on windows will always start with {{<drive letter>:\...}} so the {{:}} will always be present.

This at least affects v4 - not tested for 3.x.
h3. Steps to Reproduce

Try connecting to a MongoDB node passing the {{tlsCAFile}} query parameter in the connection string on Windows with a valid absolute path.
h3. Expected Results

The connection is created and works/fails according to the CA provided.
h3. Actual Results

No connection is created but an error is thrown while trying to execute {{fs.readFileSync}} when resolving the {{tlsCAFile}}.

summary: Remove Collection#save
description: Originally part of NODE-2317, but pulled out because a refactor of retryability would have otherwise required creating a {{SaveOperation}} just to delete it shortly thereafter.

summary: Lift restriction on authSource without credentials
description: See DRIVERS-796 for details.

summary: MongoClient.readPreference always returns primary
description:
[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/src/mongo_client.ts#L297-L299] 

 

Required for the shell's `getReadPref` and `getReadPrefTagSet`

summary: Reduce default keepalive time to align with Azure defaults
description: See DRIVERS-1228 for updated details.

summary: Throw an error if bulk update documents don't contain update operator expressions
description:
.The [internal update command|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/update/#update-command-u] can accept both:
 * A document that contains [update operator expressions|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/update/#id1],
 * A replacement document with only {{<field1>: <value1>}} pairs

The bulk command doesn't perform any additional validation, it merely passes the user-specified update document through to the internal update command.

However, it should not allow a replacement document, only documents with update operator expressions.

summary: BulkOperation.execute returns undefined for insertedIds
description:
Steps to recreate:

 
{code:java}> const bulk = c.db('test').collection('coll').initializeUnorderedBulkOp()
> for (let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) bulk.insert({x: 1}
> const result = await bulk.execute()
> result.insertedIds
-----> RETURNS { 0: undefined, ... 999: undefined }{code}
result.result.insertedIds has ids that are not undefined.

summary: Change stream should not check for NonResumableChangeStreamError label
description: The {{isResumableError}} function should not check for the {{NonResumableChangeStreamError}} label. Per the change streams spec, resumability is determined by the {{ResumableChangeStreamError}} label for servers >= 4.4 and the manually maintained whitelist for servers < 4.4.

summary: Move write statement types to respective operation file
description: The {{UpdateStatement}} and {{DeleteStatement}} types belong in the operation file for each command, not in the bulk common definitions.

summary: Move find command preparation out of query wire protocol method
description: There is a wire protocol method {{query}} which would seem to imply it only constructs {{OP_QUERY}} messages, but instead it builds find commands and uses either {{OP_MESSAGE}} or {{OP_QUERY}} to send the command. We should move the find command construction into the {{FindOperation}}, as well as the logic to use {{OP_MESSAGE}}/{{OP_QUERY}}

summary: Update Evergreen config to run the new OCSP responder
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Drivers that already have OCSP tests running in Evergreen will need to update their Evergreen config files to install the {{flask}} requirement (via a newly added requirements file) instead of {{bottle}}.  [e.g.|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/filediff/5e7cf9d71e2d174d717c3834/?file_name=evergreen%2Fevergreen.yml&patch_number=0]:
{code:bash}
-          ./venv/Scripts/pip3 install asn1crypto oscrypto bottle
+          ./venv/Scripts/pip3 install -r ${DRIVERS_TOOLS}/.evergreen/ocsp/mock-ocsp-responder-requirements.txt
{code}
 

[edit: per [~shane.harvey]'s suggestion, it would best to store the mock responder's requirements in drivers-evergreen-tools so that future updates to the mock responder's requirements will not require drivers changes]

Drivers that don't yet have OCSP tests running in Evergreen do not need to do anything, as such drivers will use {{flask}} from the get-go as  part their initial effort to get OCSP tests running in Evergreen. 

This change is needed due to: [mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools#102|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/pull/102] which fixes: [mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools#99|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/issues/99] and [mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools#100 |https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/issues/100]and includes changes from: https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/SERVER-46413
 and [mongodb/mongo#1351|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/pull/1351]

 
    See DRIVERS-977 for updated details.

summary: Add directConnection URI option assertion to SRV+directConnection=false test
description: See DRIVERS-1288 for updated details.

summary: Connecting to single mongos makes driver think it is connected to a standalone
Mark owner on DriverSessionOptions as optional
description:
I’m running into the issue that {{owner}} is not marked as optional in {{ClientSessionOptions}}, even though the user would not be expected to provide that option directly to {{startSession.}}

NOTE: if this cannot be marked as optional, please let me know what the shell needs to send for this option when calling startSession. We can store a Symbol.for('@@mongosh.shellSession') locally and pass it each time we start a session?

summary: Strict mode for `createCollection` should be removed
description: In v3.6.0 we changed erroneous behavior in {{Db.prototype.createCollection}} to look up collections by name before sending the command. The helper still supports an optional {{strict}} mode, which aside from committing the same mistake (only looking up by name, rather than name + options), offers no additional benefit over the default behavior. It should be removed.

summary: noCursorTimeout does not seem to for find()
Support speculative authentication attempts in isMaster
description: See DRIVERS-918 for details.

summary: Driver cannot find package.json in util.ts when bundled
Unify behavior around configuration for replica set discovery
Unordered bulk write aborts on first encountered error
description: A refactoring of bulk operations last summer removed a crucial check for unordered bulk operations, resulting in unordered operations failing after the first failed operation.

summary: Define behavior of connectTimeoutMS=0 with streaming protocol
description: See DRIVERS-1289 for updated details.

summary: Raise error when hint option is provided on unacknowledged writes against any server version
description: A previous version of the CRUD spec said that the driver should only raise errors when the hint option is provided to an unacknowledged write against a server that does not support the hint option. However, the CRUD spec also says that the driver should raise errors whenever the arrayFilters or collation options are provided on any unacknowledged write. The spec has been changed to state that the driver should raise an error when the hint option is provided on unacknowledged writes against any server version.

summary: BulkWriteResult and MongoBulkWriteError do not respect the values in BulkResult
description: Each time a BulkWriteResult is made, mergeBatchResults is called which merges the results from the batch into the BulkResult, this means BulkWriteResult's properties become out of sync.

summary: Setting connectionTimeoutMS to 0 will result in a disconnection every heartbeatFrequencyMS
description:
When connecting to mongoDB setting
{code:java}
connectTimeoutMS: 0{code}
the mongodb client emits a MongoNetworkTimeoutError every times the heartbeat expires. (defaults to every 10 seconds).

It could be that at

[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.6/lib/core/sdam/monitor.js#L208]

The socket timeout is equated to the heartbeat timeout, resulting in a socket failure?

summary: Simplify code path for ending implicit sessions in cursors
description: The internal {{Cursor#_endSessions}} is called from many places in the codebase, which can be confusing and also leads to bugs. We should reduce these calls to command result handlers, and the {{close}} method.

summary: Improve/consolidate options inheritance logic
description: The options inheritance logic should be refactored into a single options resolution method, which can be used by the caller as necessary before creating a command, and elsewhere.

summary: merge CommandOperation and CommandOperationV2
description: There are only a few operations still using the {{CommandOperation}}, and it's preventing us from fully removing the {{NativeTopology}}. We should migrate all these commands to use {{CommandOperationV2}}, and remove the legacy operation type.

summary: Drivers should retry replSetStepDown after "Unable to acquire X lock" error
description: See DRIVERS-1290 for updated details.
